claim_text: Whereas most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac27,28,29,34 , we hypothesized that the 13 C Lac/Bic ratio is a surrogate marker for GLY/OXPHOS5,35,36 .
manuscript_section: Introduction §1.3 (HP13C prior-art motivation for Lac/Bic hypothesis)

sub_claims.text: Nelson 2013 is an HP13C cancer study whose primary readout is the 13C-lactate signal (i.e., it focuses on Lac).

evidence.snippet: Increased signal from hyperpolarized [1-13 C]lactate has been observed in preclinical models of prostate cancer relative to normal tissues ... Levels of hyperpolarized [1- 13 C]lactate and the flux of [1-13 C]pyruvate to [1- 13 C]lactate increase with cancer progression (pathologic grade) and reduce after therapy.
the secondary aims were to evaluate the kinetics of delivery to the prostate and assess differences in [1- 13 C]lactate/[1- 13 C]pyruvate for regions of cancer versus other tissues.

These dynamic data demonstrated reproducible delivery of hyperpolarized [1- 13 C]pyruvate to the prostate and its conversion to hyperpolarized [1-13 C]lactate. ... Representative 13 C spectra ... demonstrated peaks corresponding to [1- 13 C]pyruvate [173 ppm] and [1- 13 C]lactate (185 ppm).

The purpose of the single-time point spatially localized data was to compare the relative levels of [1- 13 C]lactate and [1- 13 C]pyruvate in regions of tumor versus normal prostate and surrounding tissue.

The median [1-13 C]lactate/[1- 13 C]pyruvate ratios were 0.94 (range, 0.85 to 1.25) on the right side and 1.35 (range, 0.73 to 4.39) on the left side of the gland. The median [1-13 C]lactate/[1- 13 C]pyruvate ratio in a region of the central gland — which was not thought to include tumor — was 0.45.
The kinetics of delivering the hyperpolarized agent and the increase in [1- 13 C]lactate signal were consistent with those observed in preclinical models.

sub_claims.nuance: Nelson 2013 is a paradigmatic Lac-focused HP13C cancer study: lactate (and the Lac/Pyr ratio) is the paper's sole metabolic readout, its tumor-contrast biomarker, and the quantitative endpoint throughout Results, Discussion, and Table 2. Bic/acetyl-CoA/alanine peaks are neither measured nor mentioned. This directly supports the manuscript's premise that 'most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac'.

remediation.suggested_edit: No edit required. Nelson 2013 is an exemplary, correctly-cited reference for 'most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac'.

phrasings_tried: HP13C cancer study focused on lactate
attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Nelson 2013 is the primary first-in-human HP13C prostate cancer study; no indirect attribution concern.
attestation.out_of_context_check: The manuscript cites Nelson 2013 inside the 'most HP13C cancer studies have focused on Lac' bundle (refs 27,28,29,34), which is exactly what Nelson 2013 exemplifies. The cite is in-context.
